evals.name: contract-qa
evals.prompt: You are working in the CLIuno workspace at /home/rum/github/cliuno. I'm building a small mobile client against the CLIuno backend templates and need three precise facts: (1) what exact JSON body do the web frontends send to POST /api/v1/auth/refresh-token, (2) which key(s) must the response include for the frontends to keep working, and (3) where do the backends store the one-time password-reset token on the server side? Answer all three precisely.
evals.expected_output: States the body is {refreshToken} (camelCase), the response must include data.token (refreshed refreshToken too), and reset tokens are stored on the user row (reset_token / reset_password_token columns), not in a separate blacklist or cache.
assertions: Answer states the refresh-token request body key is refreshToken (camelCase), not refresh_token-only or a Bearer header
Answer states the response must include data.token (the access token under the data envelope)
Answer states one-time reset tokens are stored on the user record/row (reset_token, or reset_password_token for Rails/Laravel)

evals.name: debug-shape-failure
evals.prompt: I ran the CLIuno compatibility matrix in /home/rum/github/cliuno and one backend column shows: `FAIL POST /auth/login -> 200 no token in response`, even though logging in with curl returns HTTP 200 and looks fine. Explain exactly what is wrong and list the concrete things to check and fix in that backend.
evals.expected_output: Explains the matrix asserts response shapes, not just status codes: the frontends read data.token from the login response, so a 200 whose envelope lacks data.token fails. Points at the backend's login response envelope ({status, message, data:{token,...}}) as the thing to fix, referencing the shared contract.
assertions: Identifies that the HTTP status was fine and the failure is about the response body shape (envelope), not the status code
States the frontends/matrix require the access token at data.token in the login response
Directs the fix at the backend's login response envelope (add token under data), not at the frontend or at auth credentials

evals.name: run-single-column
evals.prompt: In /home/rum/github/cliuno, run the CLIuno compatibility matrix for ONLY the fastapi backend against ONLY the react frontend. Then write a short report containing: the exact command you used, how many flow steps passed, and the react/fastapi cell result. Also copy the generated JSON report file next to your report.
evals.expected_output: Runs node scripts/test-matrix.mjs --backends fastapi --frontends react (from core/), reports flow 45/45 steps passed and the react cell as PASS, and copies core/matrix-report.json into the outputs.
assertions: The report shows the matrix was invoked with both --backends fastapi and --frontends react (single column, not the full board)
The report states 45/45 flow steps passed (or N/N with all steps passing)
The report states the react x fastapi cell result is PASS
A copy of the generated matrix-report.json exists in the outputs directory
